actionable_predicate: During scheduled deep architectural work, exactly one input stream most likely to act as the wind in your sail today should be restrained for the duration of the model-loading session — not forever, just until the model is loaded. The audit predicate: 'identify the channel most likely to deflect the work; restrain that channel for this work-block; restore afterward'.
example_decision: A staff engineer is scoping a four-hour block to redesign the order-fulfillment state machine. The PM applies this verse: which channel is the wind today? The answer for this engineer this Wednesday is Slack — the rest of the team is in a high-bandwidth incident-aftermath thread that will fire notifications all afternoon. The decision: she sets Slack to do-not-disturb for the four-hour block; PagerDuty stays on; her direct manager has her phone for genuine emergencies. The verse predicts that the resulting prajñā would catch a class of defect (lock-ordering, consistency-model-load) that interrupts during model-loading would have lost. The PM tracks the audit: did the redesign land cleaner than the prior comparable effort under uninterrupted Slack? If yes, the predicate is operational; if no, the wind was not Slack — it was something else, and the audit retargets next time.
